---
name: decision-log
description: Capture high-impact project decisions with rationale and consequences. Use when the team approves a stack, scope boundary, migration approach, or other decision that should be preserved.
---

Use this skill when a planning conversation produces a meaningful decision that future contributors need to understand.

## Goal

Store decisions in a compact, reusable form so later work does not reopen settled context accidentally.

## Decision format

Capture:

- Decision
- Context
- Options considered
- Chosen option
- Why it was chosen
- Consequences or follow-up impact

## When to log a decision

Log decisions such as:

- approved stack
- major scope boundary
- milestone structure
- adoption mode
- architecture pattern
- integration strategy

## Guardrails

- Do not log unresolved debates as decisions.
- Do not duplicate full discussion transcripts.
- Keep each decision concise and link it back to the affected project brief or issue.
